name: Mem0 (Intelligent Memory for AI Agents)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0) self-hosted; Cloud API: $0.002/memory op; Free tier available

auth.notes: MEM0_API_KEY for cloud; self-hosted uses OpenAI/Anthropic keys

performance.notes: Memory retrieval via vector search; ~200ms add, ~100ms retrieval

agent_readiness.notes: Persistent memory layer for AI agents. memory.add([messages], user_id='user'). memory.search('what do they like?', user_id='user'). Extracts facts/preferences from conversations automatically. User-scoped and session-scoped memory. Supports vector stores (Qdrant, Pinecone, etc.) + graph memory. OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure compatibility. LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen integrations. Python and TypeScript SDKs. Cloud or self-hosted. Used to make LLMs remember users across sessions.

gotchas: Memory extraction quality depends on LLM used
Cloud pricing adds up at scale ($0.002/op)
best_when: AI agents needing persistent user memory across sessions
Chatbots that should remember user preferences and facts
Multi-user AI applications where each user has personalized memory
avoid_when: High-scale production (cloud costs; self-hosted needs ops)
summary: Mem0 provides persistent memory for AI agents. memory.add(messages, user_id) extracts facts automatically. memory.search(query, user_id) retrieves relevant memories. Vector + graph storage. LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen integrations. Self-hosted or cloud. Python and TypeScript SDKs. Best for AI agents needing to remember user preferences and facts across sessions. The leading open-source memory layer for agents.
